explicit_text: I was born in Dar es Salaam on April 21, 1970. It's a big city in Tanzania where I grew up and later lived in the Kurasini area. Dar es Salaam is where I started my journey, going to the Dar es Salaam Maritime Institute and working with Sea Express. It’s a place full of connections and opportunities that shaped my early career and life.

explicit_text: I come from Tanzania, a country in East Africa where Dar es Salaam is located. Growing up there, I was surrounded by a big family and a vibrant community. Tanzania is where I pursued my education at the Dar es Salaam Maritime Institute and started working on ships. It’s my homeland and the foundation of my personal and professional life before moving to the United States.

explicit_text: After finishing secondary school, I attended the Dar es Salaam Maritime Institute, or DMI, where I learned about working on ships and the ocean. Since I was a kid, I wanted to be a sailor, so DMI was the perfect place for me. It was there that I gained the skills and knowledge to work with companies like Sea Express, and it really fueled my passion for maritime work.
implicit_text: After finishing secondary school, I attended the main maritime training center in the largest Tanzanian city, where I learned about working on ships and the ocean. Since I was a kid, I wanted to be a sailor, so this institute was the perfect place for me. It was there that I gained the skills and knowledge to work with companies like the local shipping firm, and it really fueled my passion for maritime work.

explicit_text: I worked with Sea Express, a company involved with ships coming into Dar es Salaam. My job was to help prepare ships for departure and manage cargo, which I really enjoyed. Through Sea Express, I met many tourists and students, which eventually led to important connections, including meeting James Harrison and others who influenced my life.

explicit_text: James Harrison was someone I met through my work in Tanzania. He contacted me to help with his research proposal and paperwork because I had many connections in Dar es Salaam. He encouraged others coming to Tanzania to reach out to me, which led to meeting my first wife and building a strong relationship through work and life.

explicit_text: I moved to the United States after marrying my first wife, who was focusing on her Ph.D. there. It was a big change from Tanzania, leaving my large family and familiar life behind. Though it was difficult at times, with feelings of loneliness and arguments, I eventually found peace and happiness here. America became the place where I built my fitness career and life with new opportunities.

explicit_text: Kurasini is the area in Dar es Salaam where I grew up and lived during my youth. It was a vibrant neighborhood with many Chinese residents, and I learned a lot from a Chinese martial arts trainer named Iduo there. My time in Kurasini between ages 11 and 15 was when I really developed my love for martial arts and training, which has been a lifelong passion.
